mission.title: Install and Configure Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through the installation of Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE), a managed Kubernetes service on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. It simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters while providing high availability and scalability. Key trade-offs include reliance on Oracle's infrastructure and potential costs associated with cloud resources.

steps.title: Install the Oracle Cloud CLI
steps.description:
The Oracle Cloud CLI is required to interact with Oracle Cloud services. This step ensures you have the necessary tools to create and manage your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cli/master/scripts/install/install.sh | bash

oci --version

```

> This command downloads and installs the Oracle Cloud CLI.
> Verify the installation by checking the CLI version.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster in Oracle Cloud. Specify the desired Kubernetes version, node count, and region.

```bash

oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-k8s-cluster --kubernetes-version 'v1.30.2' --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "size": 3}]'

```

> Replace <your_compartment_id> and <your_vcn_id> with your actual IDs.

steps.title: Configure kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step sets up your kubeconfig file to allow kubectl to communicate with your new Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --file $HOME/.kube/config --region <your_region> --overwrite

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> and <your_region> with your actual values.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Installation Configuration
steps.description:
This step involves setting up additional features like autoscaling and monitoring for your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/deploy/oci-cloud-controller-manager.yaml

kubectl create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(oci iam user get --user-id <your_user_id> --query 'data.email' --raw-output)

```

> Deploy the OCI Cloud Controller Manager.
> Grant cluster-admin permissions to your user.
commands: kubectl create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(oci iam user get --user-id <your_user_id> --query 'data.email' --raw-output)

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --force --wait-for-state TERMINATED

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> with your actual cluster ID.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent volumes are deleted.

```bash

oci lb load-balancer delete --load-balancer-id <your_load_balancer_id> --force

```

> Replace <your_load_balancer_id> with your actual load balancer ID.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step upgrades the Kubernetes version of your cluster.

```bash

oci ce cluster update --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --kubernetes-version 'v1.30.2'

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> with your actual cluster ID.
> Verify that nodes are updated and in 'Ready' state.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create load balancer'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the specified subnets are not correctly configured or accessible.

**Fix:**
Check your subnet configurations and ensure they are in the same VCN as your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized' when running kubectl commands
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your kubeconfig is not set up correctly or your user does not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Re-run the kubeconfig setup command and ensure your user has the correct roles assigned.

troubleshooting.title: Nodes are in 'NotReady' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the node is not able to communicate with the control plane.

**Fix:**
Check the node's network configuration and ensure it has internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or Memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the requested resources exceed the available capacity in your region.

**Fix:**
Adjust the node shape or size in your cluster configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new node shapes and improved load balancer configurations.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older node shapes and configurations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.29, ensure to review the new node shape configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with nodes listed as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets: kubectl create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(oci iam user get --user-id <your_user_id> --query 'data.email' --raw-output)

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Oracle Cloud CLI installed and configured.
